name: Noël Coward
description: English playwright, composer, director, actor, and singer (1899–1973)
abstract: Sir Noël Peirce Coward (16 December 1899 – 26 March 1973) was an English playwright, composer, director, actor, and singer, known for his wit, flamboyance, and what Time called "a sense of personal style, a combination of cheek and chic, pose and poise". Coward attended a dance academy in London as a child, making his professional stage …

about_quotes.speaker: Pug Horton
about_quotes.evidence: It was mostly the popular music of the ‘30s, Noel Coward and so on.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pug Horton interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Alberta Hunter
about_quotes.evidence: Mr. Coward used to frequent the Dorchester all the time... I used to sing different songs, and I guess he just thought my voice was all right and asked me to do 'Two Cigarettes in the Dark,' which I did.

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Armstrong
about_quotes.evidence: Well, no, I wouldn't be like him. Because that's just like, all right, can you name somebody a little lesser than Noel? He's the greatest.

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Flanigan
about_quotes.evidence: Noel Coward’s “Private Lives.” ... we were supposed to be the pit band. ... They wound up using the tape rather than the live band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Flanigan interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I noticed you also included in that selection a Noel Coward song. [INTERVIEWEE] Yes.
